# Justin Johnson — Co-Founder, World Labs [Auto-Generated] 

**Twitter/X:** [@jcjohnss](https://x.com/jcjohnss)
**Personal site:** [web.eecs.umich.edu/~justincj/](https://web.eecs.umich.edu/~justincj/)
**Background:** Former Assistant Professor at University of Michigan, co-instructor of Stanford CS231N, Google Scholar h-index researcher in computer vision

## Key Themes

- **Scaling compute**
- **Scene understanding**
- **Education and accessibility** 
- **Visual reasoning**

## Key Research (Informing World Labs Product Direction)

- Scene Graphs → Structured 3D Generation
- Perceptual Quality

## Educational Impact
- **Stanford CS231N** (2015-2017, co-instructor with Fei-Fei Li) — One of the most widely viewed deep learning courses
- **UMich EECS 498-007** (2019-2022) — Evolution of CS231N with new topics: Transformers, Video, 3D. All materials freely available.
- **NSF CAREER Award** recipient

## Relevance to Product Strategy
- Scaling compute thesis implies World Labs will differentiate on **infrastructure investment** (more compute = better worlds)
